label: CoreBank Identity Verification (2022)
summary: Mid-stage Toronto vendor signed after Initech accepted a $2M aggregate cap but kept breach liability uncapped. Vendor walked away on convenience termination twice before agreeing to a 60-day cure period instead.

clauses.text: Vendor's total aggregate liability shall not exceed CAD $2,000,000; provided that the foregoing cap shall not apply to claims arising from a breach of Section 9 (Data Protection) or any unauthorized access to or disclosure of Client Data, in respect of which Vendor's liability shall be uncapped.

Client may audit Vendor's information security controls no more than twice per calendar year upon forty-eight (48) hours' prior written notice. Vendor shall bear the costs of its own personnel in connection with any such audit.

Either party may terminate upon material breach not cured within sixty (60) days of written notice. Initech declined to accept any convenience termination right in this Agreement following extensive negotiation.
Vendor shall notify Client within twenty-four (24) hours of becoming aware of any unauthorized access to Client Data, with a follow-up incident report within five (5) business days.
Client Data shall be stored and processed in Canada. Cross-border transfer requires Client's prior written consent on a per-event basis.
